title: Boat Rental in Kas: A Budget Guide for 2026
topic: a budget guide to renting a boat in Kas
html:
Kas, on Turkey's Turquoise Coast, offers affordable boat hire from the small harbour near the town centre, with half-day motorboat rentals starting around €150. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Kas or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Kas?

A budget boat rental in Kas starts at €150 for a half-day on a small motorboat. Daily rates for a private skipper range from €350 to €700, depending on vessel size and season. Shared tours can cost as little as €50 per person. One option worth a look is the Salty Sun Charters charter page.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Kas?

No, you do not need a licence to rent a boat in Kas if you hire a crewed vessel. For bareboat rentals, the operator will confirm exact licence requirements based on your nationality and boat size; foreign licences are often accepted with a translation.

Where to set off in Kas

Most boat hire in Kas departs from the main harbour, near the bust of Atatürk. The municipal marina offers a few berths for visiting yachts. Other launching points include the small beach of Büyük Çakıl and the fishing harbour of Çukurbağ Yarımadası. Popular islands and bays to visit include Kekova Island, Simena (Kaleköy), and the blue grottoes of Pigeon Island.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), ideal for exploring coves independently, from €150 half-day.
  2. Gulet, traditional Turkish sailing boat, best for day cruises to Kekova with up to 12 guests, from €500.
  3. Catamaran (Sanyati or Lagoon 46), spacious for families or groups, up to 10 guests, from €800 full-day.
  4. Crewed yacht (Azimut 40), luxury with skipper, for 8 guests, from €1,000 full-day.
  5. RIB or speedboat (Axopar 28), thrilling ride for 6 guests, perfect for water sports, from €250 half-day.

Breakdown of boat rental costs in Kas

  • Shared group tour: €50, €80 per person for a half-day to Kekova.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €150, €200 in low season.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €800, €1,200 for up to 10 guests.
  • Crewed yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 38): €1,200, €1,500 full-day, inclusive of fuel and lunch.

When to go and what to know

High season in Kas runs from June to September, when the meltemi wind can pick up in the afternoons. Spring and autumn offer calmer seas and lower prices. To rent a boat in Kas, check yacht charter in Kas for verified listings and real-time availability.

For a quiet swim, head to the secluded bay of Limanağzı, a 20-minute motor from the harbour, before the noon crowds arrive.

At a glance

  • Price range: €50, €1,500 per trip
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Kas

Can I rent a boat for a week in Kas?

Yes, weekly bareboat charters are available on larger yachts such as the Maiora 85, with prices starting from €5,000.

Is it safe to rent a boat without a skipper?

If you have boating experience and the operator approves, bareboat rental is safe; always check the weather forecast.

How far is Kekova from Kas by boat?

Kekova Island is approximately 10 nautical miles east of Kas, a 45-minute trip from the harbour.

What is included in the rental price?

Crewed rentals typically include fuel, a skipper, soft drinks, and sometimes lunch; bareboat hires exclude fuel.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Kas and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
